prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, portrait orientation
Primary request: D&D 5e / Forgotten Realms inspired heroic high fantasy world map for the terrain type dual_continent.
Scene/backdrop: two major magical continents separated by uneven enchanted seas and narrow straits, with frontier towns, noble city-states, coastal harbors, deep ancient forests, jagged mountain spines, winding rivers, scattered dungeon entrances, dragon lairs, ruined wizard towers and radiant rune-stone sites.
Style/medium: high-resolution illustrated fantasy atlas, cartographic parchment style, inked topographic relief, hand-painted terrain, heroic high fantasy campaign-map atmosphere with subtle magical glow.
Composition/framing: tall portrait map, asymmetric continents of different sizes; the sea separation is diagonal and S-shaped with C-shaped gulfs, L-shaped channels and jagged straits, not a simple vertical divider; include compass rose, decorative border, trade roads, dungeon icons and subtle boundaries without readable text.
Geography constraints: coastlines must be realistic and complex with peninsulas, bays, gulfs, capes, fjords, broken shores, curved coasts, deltas, inland seas, marshy estuaries and varied landforms; include snowy peaks, plains, forests, deserts, jungle interiors, wetlands, volcanic lands and enchanted wastelands.
Avoid: mirrored continents, clean vertical split, simple two-blob layout, modern satellite image, board-game UI, readable labels, watermark, logo, real-world map copy.
